title: वराह स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री वराह स्तोत्रम् ॥

ऋषय ऊचुः —
जितं जितं तेऽजित यज्ञभावना-
त्रयीं तनुं स्वां परिधुन्वते नमः।
यद्रोमगर्तेषु निलिल्युरध्वरा-
स्तस्मै नमः कारणसूकराय ते॥ १॥

रूपं तवैतन्ननु दुष्कृतात्मनां
दुर्दर्शनं देव यदध्वरात्मकम्।
छन्दांसि यस्य त्वचि बर्हिरोम-
स्वाज्यं दृशि त्वङ्घ्रिषु चातुर्होत्रम्॥ २॥

स्रुक्तुण्ड आसीत्स्रुव ईश नासयो-
रिडान्तरे जुह्वति सप्त जिह्वया।
समस्त्रयी चर्चिकया सुनिर्मला
समाहिता कर्णविवरेऽघमर्षणी॥ ३॥

घ्राणेन ते सर्वमयं सुसम्भृतं
नमामि यज्ञं सदनं शरीरिणाम्।
त्वमेव नित्यं परमात्मभेषजं
भवौषधं भूरि दयालुमीश्वरम्॥ ४॥

श्रीवत्ससाम्राज्यमनन्तवीर्यं
ध्रियाच गदां पद्मचतुष्टयं च।
भगवन्तमाद्यं पुरुषं पुराणं
वराहरूपं भज रे मनो मम॥ ५॥

नमो नमस्तेऽखिलयज्ञतन्तवे
स्थितौ गृहीतामलसत्त्वमूर्तये।
दृष्टं हि यन्नो धरणी विधारण-
मिमं करोत्यानतकम्परत्नम्॥ ६॥

जयजय भगवन्नाद्य वराह
क्रीडसि यदर्णवमध्यगां महीम्।
उज्जहर्थ हसदम्बुजजायां
भक्तवत्सल कृपामय जीवनाधार॥ ७॥

दंष्ट्राग्रकोट्या भगवंस्त्वया धृता
विराजते भूधरभूषणा मही।
यथा सरोजं शिशिरांशु भूषणं
तथाऽपि दंष्ट्रे तव भाति मेदिनी॥ ८॥

त्रयीमयं रूपमिदं च सौकरं
भक्तानुकम्पा विधये गृहीतम्।
येनार्तपद्मा भवसागरान्तर-
स्तापत्रयेणाभिहता समुद्धृता॥ ९॥

नमो नमस्ते भगवन्वराह
यज्ञेश यज्ञ पुरुषोत्तमाय।
सर्वात्मने सर्वविधानकर्त्रे
नमोऽस्तु ते धरणिधारणाय॥ १०॥

॥ इति श्री वराह स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Varaha Stotram ||

Rishaya Uchuh —
Jitam jitam te-ajita yajna-bhavana-
Trayim tanum svam paridhunvate namah.
Yad-roma-gartesu nililyur-adhvara-
Stasmai namah karana-sukaraya te. || 1 ||

Rupam tava-etan-nanu dushkrita-atmanam
Dur-darshanam deva yad-adhvara-atmakam.
Chhandamsi yasya tvachi barhir-oma-
Svajyam drishi tvang-ghrishu chatur-hotram. || 2 ||

Sruk-tunda asit-sruva Isha nasayor-
Ida-antare juhvati sapta jihvaya.
Samastra-yi charchikaya su-nirmala
Samahita karna-vivare-agha-marshani. || 3 ||

Ghranena te sarva-mayam su-sambhritam
Namami yajnam sadanam sharirinaam.
Tvam-eva nityam parama-atma-bheshajam
Bhava-aushadham bhuri dayalum-ishvaram. || 4 ||

Shrivatsa-samrajyam-ananta-viryam
Dhriyach-cha gadam padma-chatushtayam cha.
Bhagavantam-adyam purusham puranam
Varaha-rupam bhaja re mano mama. || 5 ||

Namo namas-te-akhila-yajna-tantave
Sthitau grihita-amala-sattva-murtaye.
Drishtam hi yan-no dharani vidharanam-
Imam karoty-anata-kampa-ratnam. || 6 ||

Jaya jaya Bhagavan-nadya Varaha
Kridasi yad-arnava-madhya-gam mahim.
Ujjahartha hasad-ambuja-jayam
Bhakta-vatsala kripa-maya jivana-adhara. || 7 ||

Damshtra-agra-kotya Bhagavams-tvaya dhrita
Virajate bhu-dhara-bhushana mahi.
Yatha sarojam shishira-amshu bhushanam
Tatha-api damshtre tava bhati medini. || 8 ||

Trayi-mayam rupam-idam cha saukaram
Bhakta-anukampa vidhaye grihitam.
Yena-arta-padma bhava-sagara-antara-
Stapa-trayena-abhihata samudhrita. || 9 ||

Namo namas-te Bhagavan Varaha
Yajnesha yajna Purushottamaya.
Sarva-atmane sarva-vidhana-kartre
Namo-astu te dharani-dharanaya. || 10 ||

|| Iti Shri Varaha Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ऋषियों ने कहा — हे अजित! आपकी जय हो, जय हो! जो यज्ञ-भावना रूपी वेदत्रयी को अपने शरीर में धारण करते हैं, उन्हें नमस्कार। जिनके रोमकूपों में यज्ञ समा गए — उन कारणभूत सूकर (वराह) को नमस्कार॥ १॥

हे देव! आपका यह यज्ञमय रूप दुष्कर्मी आत्माओं के लिए दुर्दर्शन है। आपकी त्वचा में छन्द हैं, रोम कुश हैं, दृष्टि में आज्य (घी) है, चरणों में चतुर्होत्र है॥ २॥

आपका मुख स्रुक् (हवन पात्र), नासिका स्रुव, दोनों नासिका के बीच इडा, सात जिह्वाओं से हवन करते हैं, समस्त वेदत्रयी विशुद्ध अर्चना से समाहित, कान के छिद्र में अघमर्षण (पापनाशक) है॥ ३॥

आपके दाँतों की नोक पर धरी हुई भूमि ऐसी शोभित हो रही है जैसे चन्द्रमा पर कमल। हे भगवन्! वराह रूप में पृथ्वी को समुद्र से उठाकर धारण करने वाले आपको नमस्कार॥ ८-१०॥
benefits: Glorifies Lord Vishnu's Varaha (boar) avatara who rescued the Earth
Invokes stability, protection, and grounding in life
The yajnamaya (sacrifice-embodied) form purifies all karmas
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles related to land, property, and foundation
Invokes the blessing of divine protection for the devotee's world and environment
Powerful for worship on Varaha Dvadashi
description: Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the Varaha Stotram (वराह स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. Attributed to Shrimad Bhagavata Purana, Canto 3, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to bestow divine protection for the devotee's world and environment, grant stability, protection, and grounding in life, and glorify Lord Vishnu's Varaha (boar) avatara who rescued the Earth. For best results, devotees chant this on Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ekadashi. The text contains 10 verses and takes around 7 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Varaha Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Varaha Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer to Lord Vishnu's Varaha avatar who rescued Earth from the cosmic ocean.
relatedSearches: Varaha avatar story Hiranyaksha Bhumi rescue
Yajna Varaha form Vishnu sacrifice symbolism
Varaha Jayanti Bhadrapada Shukla Tritiya date
Bhu Devi rescue ocean Rasatala story
Varaha Stotram benefits for land property

seoFaqs.question: Which Bhagavata Purana verses form the source of Varaha Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The canonical Bhu Varaha Stotram, also called Sri Yajna Varaha Stotram, is composed of verses 3.13.35 to 3.13.46 of the Srimad Bhagavata Purana, totalling twelve shlokas. These verses are uttered by the assembled rishis and devas as Lord Varaha emerges from the cosmic ocean carrying Bhumi-devi on his tusks. Each shloka identifies the Varaha form with elements of the Vedic yajna: his bristles are the kusha grass, his eyes the day and night, his tongue the agni, his ears the sruva ladle, and so on. This identification places Varaha as the embodiment of the sacrifice itself, hence the title Yajna Varaha. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Bhaktinidhi; Hindupedia Stotra Rathnas.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the narrative context of Hiranyaksha and the earth's submersion?
seoFaqs.answer: The Bhagavata Purana relates that Hiranyaksha, son of Kashyapa and Diti and elder brother of Hiranyakashipu, received a near-invincibility boon from Brahma. Drunk on power, he challenged Varuna and the devas, and in his assault on cosmic order he rolled up the earth like a mat and dragged her down into Rasatala, the lowest of the seven netherworlds, submerging her under the primordial waters. Prithvi-devi prayed to Vishnu, who emerged from Brahma's nostril as a tiny boar that grew rapidly to mountainous size, dived into Rasatala, slew Hiranyaksha in a thousand-year duel, and lifted Bhumi back to her cosmic position on his tusks. The stotram celebrates this rescue moment. (Sources: Grokipedia Varaha; Bhaktinidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Varaha called Yajna Varaha and identified with the sacrifice?
seoFaqs.answer: The verses of the stotram systematically map every part of the boar's body to a component of the Vedic yajna: his four feet are the four Vedas, his tusks are the sacrificial stake (yupa), his tongue is the sacred fire, his head is the brahmana priest, his breath is the wind that fans the flames, and the kusha grass on the altar is his hair. This is consistent with the Vedic doctrine that yajna is itself the supreme principle through which the cosmos is sustained; Vishnu as Yajna Varaha therefore embodies the sacrifice in living form. The act of rescuing the earth becomes a cosmic yajna restoring dharmic order. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Sri Varaha Stotram; Hindupedia Maha Bhagawatha.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Varaha Stotram different from Varaha Sahasranama and Varaha Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The three texts serve complementary functions. The Varaha Stotram is the short twelve-verse Bhagavata praise-hymn focused on the rescue and yajna-symbolism. The Varaha Sahasranama, preserved in the Skanda Purana, lists one thousand names of Varaha covering every iconographic and theological aspect, and is recited on Varaha Jayanti and during land or property rituals. The Varaha Kavacham, also from the Skanda Purana, is the protective armor-prayer in which Varaha is asked to guard each direction and limb of the devotee. Practitioners typically combine all three: the stotram for daily praise, the sahasranama for special occasions, and the kavacham for protection. (Sources: archive.org Varaha Sahasranama; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of Adi Varaha Swami at Tirumala?
seoFaqs.answer: The Adi Varaha Swami temple on the banks of Swami Pushkarini at Tirumala is the oldest sanctum on the Tirumala hill and traditionally pre-dates the main Venkateswara temple. Tirumala sthala-purana relates that Vishnu first established himself on the hill in his Varaha avatar and only later granted permission to Venkateswara to settle there, on the condition that all pilgrims first take darshan of Adi Varaha before approaching Venkateswara. Although this rule is not always followed by modern pilgrims, the Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams continue to perform daily archana with Varaha Stotram at the Adi Varaha shrine, anchoring the hymn within one of the most visited pilgrim sites of India. (Sources: Bhaktinidhi; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do devotees seek by reciting Varaha Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional commentaries enumerate land-related blessings as the principal phala: acquisition of agricultural land, recovery of lost property, success in real-estate transactions, and the laying of foundations for new homes are all said to be aided by Varaha worship, since Varaha is the deity who lifted the earth herself. Beyond material benefits, the stotram is recited for protection against demonic interference and powerful enemies, removal of curses that drag one into low circumstances analogous to Bhumi's submersion, and the cultivation of fierce courage tempered by service. Many devotees recite Varaha Stotram before signing land documents or before the bhumi-puja that precedes new construction. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Bhaktinidhi; Hindupedia Stotra Rathnas.)
